2605.07926.title: AgentEscapeBench: Evaluating Out-of-Domain Tool-Grounded Reasoning in LLM Agents
2605.07926.paper_summary: As LLM-based agents increasingly rely on external tools, it is important to evaluate their ability to sustain tool-grounded reasoning beyond familiar workflows and short-range interactions. We introduce AgentEscapeBench, an escape-room-style benchmark that tests whether agents can infer, execute, and revise novel tool-use procedures under explicit long-range dependency constraints. Each task defines a directed acyclic dependency graph over tools and items, requiring agents to invoke real external functions, track hidden state revealed incrementally, propagate intermediate results, and submit a deterministically verifiable final answer. AgentEscapeBench includes 270 instances across five difficulty tiers and supports fully automated evaluation. Experiments with sixteen LLM agents and human participants show that performance drops sharply as dependency depth increases: humans decline from 98.3% success at difficulty-5 to 80.0% at difficulty-25, while the best model drops from 90.0% to 60.0%. Trajectory analysis attributes model failures mainly to breakdowns in long-range state tracking, clue adherence, and intermediate-result propagation. These findings suggest that current agents can often handle local tool use but still struggle with deep contextual dependencies. We hope AgentEscapeBench can serve as a diagnostic testbed for measuring current agent capabilities and informing future training efforts toward more robust general-purpose reasoning, action, and adaptation.
2605.07926.paper_summary_zh: 隨著基於 LLM 的代理越來越依賴外部工具，評估它們在熟悉的工作流程和短期互動之外維持工具基礎推理的能力變得重要。我們介紹了 AgentEscapeBench，這是一個逃脫室風格的基準，測試代理是否能在明確的長期依賴約束下推斷、執行和修訂新穎的工具使用程序。每個任務定義了一個工具和項目之間的有向無環依賴圖，要求代理調用真實的外部函數，跟踪逐步揭示的隱藏狀態，傳播中間結果，並提交一個可確定性驗證的最終答案。AgentEscapeBench 包含 270 個實例，分為五個難度級別，並支持完全自動化評估。對十六個 LLM 代理和人類參與者的實驗顯示，隨著依賴深度的增加，性能急劇下降：人類在難度 5 的成功率從 98.3% 下降到難度 25 的 80.0%，而最佳模型則從 90.0% 降至 60.0%。軌跡分析將模型失敗主要歸因於長期狀態跟踪、線索遵循和中間結果傳播的崩潰。這些發現表明，目前的代理通常能夠處理局部工具使用，但在深層上下文依賴方面仍然存在困難。我們希望 AgentEscapeBench 能夠作為一個診斷測試平台，用於測量當前代理的能力，並為未來的訓練工作提供指導，以實現更強大的通用推理、行動和適應能力。
2605.07926.author: Zhengkang Guo et.al.
2605.07926.authors: Zhengkang Guo, Yiyang Li, Lin Qiu, Xiaohua Wang, Jingwen Xv, Dongyu Ru, Xiaoyu Li, Xiaoqing Zheng, Xuezhi Cao, Xunliang Cai
